content:
30 writing prompts organized by level. Write each one, then compare with the templates in email-templates.md.
A1 — Text Message Replies (5 prompts) Prompt 1 Nachricht: “Hallo Andrei! Hast du am Samstag Zeit? Wir machen ein Grillfest im Park.”
Write a reply (2–3 sentences). Mention your weekend plans (sport, Freundin, etc.).
Prompt 2 Nachricht: “Hi Andrei! Wo treffen wir uns morgen zum Volleyball?”
Write a reply — suggest a meeting place and time in Zürich.
Prompt 3 Nachricht: “Hey, kannst du mir bitte etwas vom Coop mitbringen? Danke!”
Write a reply — confirm or say you can’t (you’re still at the office).
Prompt 4 Nachricht: “Kommst du heute zum Deutschkurs? Um 18 Uhr.”
Write a reply — you have a late meeting at SMG and can’t come.
Prompt 5 Nachricht: “Hallo Andrei! Ich habe eine neue Telefonnummer: 079 345 67 89. Speicher sie bitte!”
Write a reply — confirm and ask how the person is doing.
A2 — Simple Emails (10 prompts) Prompt 6: Cancel a doctor’s appointment You have an appointment on Thursday at 10:00. You can’t come because you have to work. Write an email: apologize, give a reason, ask for a new appointment.
Prompt 7: Ask about a course You want to take a swimming course. Write to the Schwimmbad: ask about days, times, and price.
Prompt 8: Respond to an invitation Your neighbor in Zürich invites you and your girlfriend to a Hausfest on Saturday. Write: accept the invitation, ask what you should bring, ask about the time.
Prompt 9: Cancel a reservation You reserved a table at a restaurant in Zürich for Friday evening with your girlfriend. You can’t come because of a work event. Write to the restaurant: cancel, apologize, ask if you can come next week instead.
Prompt 10: Report a problem Your internet doesn’t work. Write to the provider: describe the problem, say since when, ask for help.
Prompt 11: Ask about lost item You left your bag on the bus. Write to the transport company: describe the bag, say which bus and when, ask if they found it.
Prompt 12: Reply to a job ad You see a job ad for a data analyst (80%, flexible). Write: say you’re interested, mention your Data Science experience at SMG, ask about the team and when you can start.
Prompt 13: Thank someone Your German teacher helped you a lot. Write a thank-you email: say what you learned, thank them, wish them well.
Prompt 14: Ask about opening hours You want to visit the Ausländeramt. Write: ask about opening hours, whether you need an appointment, and what documents to bring.
Prompt 15: Complain about a product You ordered shoes online and they are the wrong size. Write to the shop: explain the problem, ask for an exchange or refund.
B1 — Request / Inquiry Emails (10 prompts) Prompt 16: Ask for vacation Write to your manager at SMG. Ask for 5 days vacation from 15–19 July. Give a reason (visiting your family in Chișinău, Moldova). Offer to finish your work before you leave.
Prompt 17: Excuse from a work event Write to the organizer of a team event at SMG. You can’t attend the team dinner on Tuesday because you have a German course. Explain why. Ask if there will be another team event soon.
Prompt 18: Ask about a German course Write to a language school. Ask about: levels available, prices, schedule, start dates, class size.
Prompt 19: Request a reference letter Write to your former employer. Ask for a reference letter (Arbeitszeugnis). Explain that you need it for a new job application. Ask how long it will take.
Prompt 20: Reschedule a job interview Write to HR. Your interview is on Monday at 9:00. You can’t come — you have a business trip that was just scheduled. Apologize. Suggest two alternative dates.
Prompt 21: Report a noise problem Write to your landlord. Your neighbors are very loud every evening after 22:00. Describe the noise. Ask the landlord to speak to them.
Prompt 22: Ask about an apartment You and your girlfriend found an apartment ad in Zürich online. Write to the landlord: ask about the exact rent, Nebenkosten, availability date, and whether the apartment is suitable for two people. Ask for a viewing appointment.
Prompt 23: Request training/Weiterbildung Write to your manager at SMG. You want to take a 3-day machine learning course. Explain why it would help your Data Science work. Ask if the company will pay for it.
Prompt 24: Cancel a membership Write to your gym (Fitnessstudio) in Zürich. You want to cancel your membership because you’re moving to a new apartment in a different part of the city and the gym is too far away. Ask about the Kündigungsfrist. Ask if you’ll get a refund for the remaining months.
Prompt 25: Ask about a German course Write to a language school in Zürich. You want to take an intensive German course (B1/B2 level). Ask about the schedule, price, and whether they have evening classes that work with a full-time job.
B2 — Opinion / Pro-Contra Texts (5 prompts) Prompt 26: Sunday shopping Sollten Geschäfte am Sonntag geöffnet sein? Write ~200 words: arguments for and against, your opinion with reasons.
Prompt 27: Home office Sollten Arbeitnehmer das Recht auf Home-Office haben? Write ~200 words: advantages and disadvantages, your conclusion.
Prompt 28: Smartphones for children Ab welchem Alter sollten Kinder ein Smartphone haben? Write ~200 words: pros and cons, your recommendation.
Prompt 29: Public transport vs. cars Sollte man den öffentlichen Verkehr gratis machen, um die Umwelt zu schützen? Write ~200 words: arguments for and against, your opinion.
Prompt 30: Workplace training Sollten Arbeitgeber Weiterbildungen für alle Mitarbeitenden bezahlen? Write ~200 words: advantages and disadvantages, your position.
Progress Tracker # Prompt Date Done Self-Rating (1–5) Notes 1 Text reply — Grillfest 2 Text reply — Volleyball 3 Text reply — Coop 4 Text reply — late meeting 5 Text reply — phone number 6 Cancel doctor appt 7 Swimming course 8 Neighbor Hausfest 9 Cancel restaurant 10 Internet problem 11 Lost bag 12 Data analyst ad reply 13 Thank teacher 14 Ausländeramt hours 15 Wrong shoe size 16 Vacation to Moldova 17 Work event excuse 18 German course inquiry 19 Reference letter 20 Reschedule interview 21 Noise complaint 22 Apartment inquiry (Zürich) 23 ML course request 24 Cancel gym (moving) 25 German course Zürich 26 Sunday shopping 27 Home office 28 Kids & smartphones 29 Free public transport 30 Workplace training

title: Writing Practice Prompts

content:
Core Principle: Complete All Parts of the Prompt The #1 mistake in SDS writing: not answering all parts of the prompt. If the prompt says “apologize, explain why, suggest a new date” — you MUST do all three.
Strategy 1: Read the Prompt Twice Before writing:
Read the prompt once to understand the situation Read it again and count the tasks (how many things you must do) Mentally check off each task as you write Example prompt: “Write an email to your boss. Ask for vacation. Say when and how long. Give a reason.” → Tasks: (1) ask for vacation (2) say when (3) say how long (4) give a reason = 4 items to include
Strategy 2: Use a Template For emails, always use the same structure:
Anrede (Greeting) ↓ Einleitung (Why you're writing — 1 sentence) ↓ Hauptteil (Main content — answer all prompt tasks) ↓ Schluss (Closing sentence — thank you or request for reply) ↓ Gruss (Sign-off) This structure works for A2, B1, AND B2 — just with more complexity at higher levels.
Strategy 3: Keep Sentences Short At A2/B1 level:
1 idea per sentence Use simple connectors: und, aber, weil, deshalb, dann Avoid long subordinate clauses if you’re not confident At B2 level:
Combine sentences with connectors: obwohl, trotzdem, einerseits/andererseits Use relative clauses: Die Firma, bei der ich arbeite, bietet Weiterbildungen an. Show range: alternate between simple and complex sentences Strategy 4: Formal vs. Informal Know which register to use:
Formal (Sie) Informal (du) Employer, landlord, school, authority Friend, close colleague, family Sehr geehrte/r… Liebe/r… or Hallo… Könnten Sie… / Ich würde gerne… Kannst du… / Ich möchte… Mit freundlichen Grüssen Liebe Grüsse / Bis bald When in doubt, use formal. It’s never wrong to be too polite.
Strategy 5: Pro/Contra Structure (B2) For opinion texts, use this template:
Paragraph 1: Introduction — state the topic and why it's relevant Paragraph 2: Arguments FOR (2 points with examples) Paragraph 3: Arguments AGAINST (2 points with examples) Paragraph 4: Your opinion + conclusion Target: ~200 words. Practice until you can write this in 15–20 minutes.
Common Mistakes to Avoid Mistake Fix Forgetting the greeting Always start with Sehr geehrte/r… or Liebe/r… Forgetting the sign-off Always end with Freundliche Grüsse + your name Not answering all parts Count the tasks in the prompt before writing Wrong register Formal for authority/employer, informal for friends No connectors Use at least 3 connectors per email Noun not capitalized ALL German nouns start with a capital letter Missing umlauts Practice typing ä, ö, ü on a tablet keyboard Practice Schedule Type Frequency Target Short emails (A2) 2x per week 20 total before exam Job-related emails (B1) 2x per week 10 total before exam Pro/contra texts (B2) 1x per week 5 total before exam Form filling (pre-A1/A1) 3x total Comfortable with all fields Text message replies (A1) 5x total Quick and natural
